project_type: Custom AI Operating System in C++
build_system: Make with clang++ (Makefile in project root). Entry point: system/mk_entry.cpp. Uses #include to pull in .cpp files directly (header-less include pattern).
test_framework: No formal test framework. Validation is via successful compilation with make all.
test_command: make all (compilation IS the test - if it compiles, it works)
verification_instructions: Run make all from MK_OS/ directory. If binary mk_os is produced with no errors, the build is good.
snapshot_or_generated_files: bootloader/build_work/ contains pre-built ISOs and qcow2 images. Do not modify.
setup_instructions: No setup needed beyond having clang++ with C++17 support. External deps: libcurl, libsqlite3.
environment_constraints: OPEN_INTERNET - full network access available. No Docker needed. Build with make all.
contribution_requirements: All .cpp files use #ifndef include guards. Knowledge files use format: source|relation|target|weight. No external libraries for AI core.
key_patterns: 1) .cpp files with #ifndef guards included directly via #include. 2) Knowledge files are .mk format (pipe-delimited). 3) Classes use MK prefix (MKPatternGraph, MKSearchEngine, etc). 4) system/mk_entry.cpp is the unified entry point that #includes other modules. 5) The Makefile finds all .cpp files except those in tools/ and standalone mains.
directory_structure: Kernel/ (OS core), ai_core/ (intelligence), ai_core/hre/ (hybrid reasoning engine), ai_core/hre/knowledge_files/ (.mk knowledge), mk_brain/ (long-term memory), network/ (internet), system/ (entry/boot/shell), tools/ (standalone utils), plugins/ (extensions), knowledge/ (loader), datasets/ (data mgmt), bootloader/ (VM images)
